questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider a cubic, $f(x)=a x^3+b x^2+c x+4, a, b, c \in R$ and $f^{\prime \prime}\left(\frac{-2}{3}\right)=0, \quad f^{\prime}(0)=3$,
$f^{\prime}\left(\frac{-2}{3}\right)=\frac{5}{3}$, and $g=f^{-1}$, then:
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & f^{\prime}(x)=3 a x^2+2 b x+c \quad \Rightarrow \quad f^{\prime}(0)=c=3 \\ & f^{\prime \prime}(x)=6 a x+2 b \Rightarrow f^{\prime \prime}\left(\frac{-2}{3}\right)=-4 a+2 b=0 \Rightarrow b=2 a\end{aligned}$
$f^{\prime}\left(\frac{-2}{3}\right)=3 a \times \frac{4}{9}+4 a \cdot\left(\frac{-2}{3}\right)+3=\frac{5}{3}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \frac{4 a}{3}=\frac{4}{3} \Rightarrow a=1, b=2 \\ & \therefore \quad f(x)=x^3+2 x^2+3 x+4\end{aligned}$
$\frac{d}{d x}(g(x) \cdot f(g(x)))=\frac{d}{d x}(x g(x))=x g^{\prime}(x)+g(x)$
$\begin{aligned} & \because \quad f(0)=4 \Rightarrow g(4)=0 \\ & \text { and } g^{\prime}(f(x))=\frac{1}{f^{\prime}(x)} \Rightarrow g^{\prime}(4)=\frac{1}{f^{\prime}(0)}=\frac{1}{3}\end{aligned}$
$\left.\therefore \quad(g(x) \cdot f(g(x)))^{\prime}\right|_{x=4}=\frac{4}{3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $y=\sqrt{x+\sqrt{x+\sqrt{x+\sqrt{x+\ldots \ldots}}}}$, where $x>0$, then $\frac{d y}{d x}$ can be:
questions.solution:
$y=\sqrt{x+y} \quad \Rightarrow \quad y^2=x+y$
$\therefore \quad y^2-y-x=0$ ...(1)
$(2 y-1) y^{\prime}=1$
$\Rightarrow \quad y^{\prime}=\frac{1}{2 y-1} \quad \Rightarrow$(a)
From equation (1), $2 y=1+\sqrt{1+4 x}$
Hence,
$2 y-1=\sqrt{1+4 x}$
$\Rightarrow \quad y^{\prime}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+4 x}} \quad \Rightarrow$(c)
On dividing by $y$, equation (1) gives
$y-1=\frac{x}{y} \quad \Rightarrow \quad 2 y-2=\frac{2 x}{y}$
(d) : $\frac{y}{2 x+y}=\frac{1}{\frac{2 x}{y}+1}=\frac{1}{(2 y-2)+1}=\frac{1}{2 y-1}$ which is same as (a).
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
